article_title: First Lab-Grown Oesophagus Restores Swallowing in Pigs Without Immunosuppression, Paving the Way for Pediatric Trials Within Five Years

content_preview.title: First Lab-Grown Oesophagus Restores Swallowing in Pigs Without Immunosuppression, Paving the Way for Pediatric Trials Within Five Years
content_preview.summary: UCL and Great Ormond Street Hospital scientists built a functional oesophagus from a donor scaffold and the recipient's own cells, restoring normal eating in eight pigs within six months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Scientists at University College London and Great Ormond Street Hospital (GOSH) have created the first laboratory-grown oesophagus that can safely replace a full section of the organ and restore normal swallowing in a growing animal, according to a study [published in Nature Biotechnology](https://www.nature.com/articles/s41587-026-03043-1) on March 20, 2026. The engineered tissue integrated fully within three months, developed functional muscle, nerves, and blood vessels by the six...
